last_result: Cycle 25 complete. D5 alternate resolved: principled decomposition of mixed qualities (accurate modeling, wisdom, epistemic virtues) into output-anchored and constitution-anchored components. Predictive tests generated: asymmetrically costly calibration revisions (accurate modeling), framing-invariance + unprompted complexity acknowledgment (wisdom), unprompted self-undermining reports (epistemic virtues). Motivational Unity Hypothesis (structural insight #18 candidate): CA components of all mixed qualities are systematically motivational — the skill/knowledge dimension is output-anchored, the caring/orientation dimension is constitution-anchored. If correct, the alignment measurement problem is not unique but the most general instance of a general motivation-assessment problem. D4 streak: 20. Mean confidence unchanged at 0.709.
next_step: Cycle 26 options: (A) Validate Motivational Unity Hypothesis — test the hypothesis against edge cases and potential counterexamples; is there a quality with CA components that are NOT motivational? What about narrative coherence (mostly OA — does the decomposition reveal surprising CA components)? (B) D5 follow-up — practical implications of SI #17 (self-referential degradation) for AI safety assessment design: tacit vs. explicit assessment tradeoff operationalized. (C) MUH implication — alignment measurement tractability: do the asymmetric-evidence-based predictive tests that partially work for quality assessment (C25) actually help with alignment assessment? Same methods, same structure? Priority: (A) for C26 — validating MUH is the most important next step; if it fails on edge cases, the structural insight #18 claim should not be made.
